name: Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120)
name_original: Jorrios y Armañón desde Basinagre — circular por el Parque Natural de Armañón
description: Hike the moderate 8.7-mile Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120) in Armañón Natural Park, featuring panoramic views and historical sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Trucios-Turtzioz offers exploration of a diverse and rugged natural landscape in the Basque Country. The municipality, located in the Biscay province, is characterized by its strongly accidented terrain and surrounding mountain ranges, including the imposing Los Jorrios (847 m) and Armañón (854 m) peaks. The Armañón Natural Park provides extensive opportunities for outdoor activity, while the Agüera River offers more gentle routes through the local scenery. This region features a network of trails suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails in Trucios-Turtzioz The most popular hiking route is Jorrios y Armañón desde Basinagre — circular por el Parque Natural de Armañón, a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route traverses the Armañón Natural Park, connecting two prominent peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sorrondegi Summit (666 m) – Armañón Summit loop from El Suceso, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This loop connects two summits, offering views of the mountainous terrain. Local hikers also love the Jorrios Summit – Armañón Summit loop from Trucios-Turtzioz, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through the mountainous landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Trucios-Turtzioz is defined by mountainous terrain, the Armañón Natural Park, and routes along the Agüera River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Trucios-Turtzioz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Trucios-Turtzioz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Trucios-Turtzioz offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 130 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Yes, Trucios-Turtzioz has over 35 easy hiking routes. An example of a more gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24639068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de las Nieves – Pico de las Nieves (Cantabria) loop from Castro Lucio&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.2 km trail. The area also features walks along the Agüera River, which are generally less strenuous.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: The terrain in Trucios-Turtzioz is predominantly mountainous and rugged, characterized by the imposing peaks of Los Jorrios (847 m) and Armañón (854 m). You'll find routes traversing these mountains, as well as paths through the Armañón Natural Park. For a different experience, there are also more gentle walks along the Agüera River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Yes, many routes in Trucios-Turtzioz are circular. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056346207/jorrios-y-armanon-desde-basinagre-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-armanon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorrios y Armañón desde Basinagre — circular por el Parque Natural de Armañón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14 km loop that connects two prominent peaks within the natural park.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the mountains and the Armañón Natural Park, you can discover several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Roque Hermitage on Mount Kolitza&lt;/a&gt; offers picturesque views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4114848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Pando Summit and Bunker&lt;/a&gt; provides a historical point of interest. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunel de la Galerna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse and rugged natural landscape, the challenging mountain ascents, and the scenic beauty of the Armañón Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that connect multiple summits in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Yes, several routes offer the chance to summit multiple peaks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10511116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorrondegi Summit (666 m) – Armañón Summit loop from El Suceso&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.8 km path that connects two significant summits, providing expansive views of the mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the Armañón Natural Park?
A: Absolutely. The Armañón Natural Park is a central feature of the region's hiking. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056346207/jorrios-y-armanon-desde-basinagre-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-armanon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorrios y Armañón desde Basinagre — circular por el Parque Natural de Armañón&lt;/a&gt; specifically traverse this stunning park, offering ample opportunities for exploration amidst its beautiful landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites that can be incorporated into a hike?
A: Yes, the region offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. The Church of San Pedro de Romaña and the San Roque hermitage are examples of historical sites situated in picturesque locations that can be included in your walks, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: What is the best way to experience the mountainous landscape of Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: To fully experience the mountainous landscape, consider routes that ascend the prominent peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10511167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorrios Summit – Armañón Summit loop from Trucios-Turtzioz&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, leading you through the heart of the region's rugged, elevated terrain.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a river in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Yes, for a different perspective of the local scenery, you can find more gentle strolls along the Agüera River. These routes offer a contrast to the mountainous terrain and provide a tranquil walking experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120)?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this circular route is about 4 hours and 40 minutes. This duration is for the full 13.2-kilometer loop, assuming a moderate hiking pace.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Los Jorrios Trail is classified as medium-high difficulty (Ertaina-handia). While some parts have a gradual ascent, it can also be persistently steep, requiring a reasonable level of fitness. It might be challenging for absolute beginners, but those with some hiking experience and good physical condition should find it rewarding.

faqs.question: Where does the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120) start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular mountain route that typically starts from the urban center of Trucios-Turtzioz in Bizkaia. While the research doesn't specify dedicated parking, starting from the town center usually implies public parking options are available nearby. It's always a good idea to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You will traverse varied terrain, including lush forests, magical karstic areas, and ancient holm oak groves. The paths can be uneven, following ancient routes once used by shepherds and smugglers. Sturdy hiking boots are highly recommended for the diverse surfaces.

faqs.question: What notable features and viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, especially from the elevated sections. You'll pass through old villages, ancient stone structures, palaces, tower houses, and old mills, showcasing the region's cultural heritage. The route also ascends to two prominent peaks: Jorrios Summit and Armañón Summit.

faqs.question: Is the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether the trail is dog-friendly or any specific leash rules. Given its location within the Armañón Natural Park, it's advisable to check local park regulations regarding pets before your visit to ensure compliance and protect local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in Armañón Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The research does not mention any specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Los Jorrios Trail or accessing Armañón Natural Park. However, there are conservation restrictions: access between Basinagre and Campapedreo is restricted from March 1st to September 15th to protect local flora and fauna. Always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, be aware of specific conservation restrictions. Access between Basinagre and Campapedreo is limited from March 1st to September 15th. For the best experience, consider visiting outside this period or planning your route to avoid the restricted section. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, but mountain weather can change rapidly, so be prepared with layers.

faqs.question: What wildlife and natural diversity can I observe on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail showcases rich biodiversity, with lush forests and opportunities to spot local wildlife, particularly various bird species. The unique karstic landscapes and ancient holm oak groves also contribute to its natural appeal. Remember to observe wildlife from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Does the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120) connect with other well-known hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, part of the ascent to Los Jorrios is also marked as a GR route. Specifically, it overlaps with sections of the Bizkaiko bira (GR-123, stage 18). The route also incorporates parts of the Los Jorrios ibilbidea and Senda de los Jorrios (PR-BI 120).

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's medium-high difficulty and varied terrain, you should be prepared with adequate water, sturdy hiking boots for uneven surfaces, and layers of clothing for changing mountain weather. Carrying a reliable navigation tool is also recommended, even though the path is generally well-marked with yellow and white PR trail markers.
